turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure follow-up. I’ve also been getting these on-and-off headaches for the last few weeks.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Hello, thanks for coming in. We’ll go through both. How have your blood pressure readings been at home, if you’ve been checking them?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I have checked a few times. They seem a bit up and down. Mostly around the 140s over 90s, sometimes a bit lower.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. And are you taking your amlodipine every day, or have there been any missed doses?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Mostly yes. I may have missed one or two doses when work was hectic, but not often.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s helpful. Tell me about the headaches. Where do you feel them, and what do they feel like?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: It’s usually across my forehead or at the back of my head. More like a dull ache than a sharp pain.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do they come with blurred vision, nausea, weakness, chest pain, shortness of breath, or any numbness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. Just the headache. Sometimes I feel a bit tense and tired after a long day at the desk.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do you notice anything that triggers them, like stress, poor sleep, dehydration, or skipping meals?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Probably stress and staring at the screen. I also don’t always drink enough water during the day.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Any fever, recent illness, head injury, or headaches waking you from sleep?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, nothing like that. They don’t wake me up. They just come and go.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good. How has your diet been, and are you using much salt, takeaway food, or alcohol on weekends?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I do have some takeaway, maybe twice a week. I drink socially on weekends, but not a lot. I know I probably need to walk more too.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s fine, and it’s good you’re already walking sometimes. Any side effects from amlodipine, like ankle swelling, flushing, or dizziness?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No swelling or dizziness. I’ve been okay on it.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. From what you’ve told me, this sounds more like blood pressure that is still a little above target, with headaches that may be tension or stress related rather than a dangerous cause. I’d like to check your blood pressure here today, review your home readings, and then we can decide if your medication needs adjusting. In the meantime, keep taking the amlodipine daily, try to hydrate, and limit salt and caffeine. If the headaches become sudden and severe, or you get chest pain, weakness, vision changes, or shortness of breath, seek urgent care. Otherwise, let’s plan follow-up after today’s reading and a short home log.
